qa_pairs.question: Where should User-Defined Functions (UDFs) be placed on an Alliance cluster for Ansys Fluent simulations?
qa_pairs.answer: User-Defined Functions (UDFs) should be placed in the same directory as your journal, case (.cas), and data (.dat) files on the Alliance cluster.

qa_pairs.question: How are User-Defined Functions (UDFs) integrated into an Ansys Fluent simulation through the journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: You must add a command for the User-Defined Function (UDF) into your journal file. This command should be placed before any commands that read in your simulation's case (.cas) and data (.dat) files.

qa_pairs.question: What is a crucial pre-upload step for Fluent case files that utilize User-Defined Functions (UDFs) on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Before uploading your Fluent case (.cas) file to the Alliance, you must ensure that neither the Interpreted UDFs Dialog Box nor the UDF Library Manager Dialog Box are configured to use any UDFs. This ensures that only the journal file commands control the UDFs when jobs are submitted.
